items.prompt: A design review covers Mixed nested delimiters. Select the invariant for delimiter validation. Which statement gives the decisive justification?
constraints: Use the exact output, strictness, scan direction, and tie semantics stated in the prompt.
Do not classify the algorithm from a variable name or one intermediate snapshot.

options.text: The stack contains exactly the unmatched openers in encounter order; top is the innermost unmatched opener.
The stack values must be numerically monotone.
A plausible result on the sample trace is sufficient; initialization, preservation, safe removal, and terminal cases need no proof.
The maintained order affects speed only, so correctness can be argued without explaining what retained entries mean or when answers become final.

feedback.reason: The stack contains exactly the unmatched openers in encounter order; top is the innermost unmatched opener. This is the decisive boundary for the stated output and retained-state semantics.

The stack contains exactly the unmatched openers in encounter order; top is the innermost unmatched opener. The tempting claim—The stack values must be numerically monotone.—fails because it does not establish the semantic invariant that makes removal or retention safe. Transfer only after stating the invariant and proving initialization, preservation, answer timing, safe removal, and terminal handling for the changed contract.
